title: callaeum septentrionale
intro: Callaeum septentrionale, also known as the northern Madagascar velvet apple or the velvet apple, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is a shrub that is native to Madagascar and is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Callaeum septentrionale include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat various infections, making it a valuable resource in traditional medicine. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and stems can be used to make infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
